rulings.Case Title: ARROWOOD INDEMNITY COMPANY VS. GARIN L EKMEKJIAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 24, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT GARIN EKMEKJIAN's MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. The motion is granted. Defendant demonstrates that she lacked actual notice of the lawsuit and relief is warranted under CCP 473.5. The reply includes a proposed pleading. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LITWAK & ASSOCIATES, P.C., A CALIFORNIA et al VS. NICHOLAS K EVANS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Req For Demand For Production Of Docs And To Produce Docs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 24, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT NICHOLAS EVANS Mtn To Compel Responses To Req For Demand For Production Of Docs And To Produce Docs Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part and denied in part. It is granted as to Demand Number 22. Plaintiffs shall produce all documents requested by Demand Number 22 by January 14, 2013. Plaintiffs have not proven that it would be unduly burdensome to produce them. The motion to compel is denied as to all other Demands without prejudice on grounds it is moot as to the other Demands because plaintiffs say the documents have been produced and defendant does not at this time provide any evidence to the contrary. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: YAN KALIKA et al VS. MICHAEL SCHIMMEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Eric Roe At Deposition, Production Of Business Of Records, For Determination Of Concept
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 24, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF YAN KALIKA, AA CONDOS LLC, Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Eric Roe At Deposition, Production Of Business Of Records, For Determination Of Concept Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Defendant Schimmel's request to continue the hearing is denied. The court doesn't know to what date to continue the motion because defendant Schimmel doesn't say to what date he wants the motion continued and whether the court and plaintiffs will be available on the date proposed by defendant Schimmel. Plaintiffs' motions to compel are granted in part. Eric Roe is ordered to produce by January 11, 2013, to the plaintiffs' attorney at 235 Montgomery Street, Suite 824, San Francisco, CA, all documents requested in the deposition subpoenas and notices served on him except as to any requested documents to which either he or defendant Schimmel files a motion to quash by January 11, 2013. Eric Roe is ordered to appear for his deposition at 235 Montgomery Street, Suite 824, San Francisco, CA, no later than January 21, 2013. Not later than January 4, 2013, the parties shall meet and confer as to the exact date and time of the deposition and inform the court in writing either of the date and time upon which they agreed, or that they haven't agreed and on what date and time each of them wants the deposition to proceed. If the parties do not agree upon a date and time and so inform the court by January 4, 2013, then the court will select a date and time. Sanctions are denied. The court believes that a better meet and confer effort by both sides would likely have avoided this motion over scheduling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: YAN KALIKA et al VS. MICHAEL SCHIMMEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Michael Schimmel At Deposition, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 24, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF YAN KALIKA, AA CONDOS LLC, Notice Of Motion And Omtion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Michael Schimmel At Deposition, And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Schimmel's request to continue the hearing is denied. The court doesn't know to what date to continue the motion because defendant Schimmel doesn't say to what date he wants the motion continued and whether the court and plaintiffs will be available on the date proposed by defendant Schimmel. Plaintiffs' motions to compel are granted in part. Defendant Schimmel is ordered to submit any objections to the documents requested in his deposition notice and to produce the documents to which he doesn't object that are requested in the deposition notice by January 11, 2013, to the plaintiffs' attorney at 235 Montgomery Street, Suite 824, San Francisco, CA. Defendant Schimmel is ordered to appear for his deposition at Drobny Law Offices, Inc., 4180 Truxel Road, Suite 100, Sacramento, CA 95834 no later than January 21, 2013. Not later than January 4, 2013, the parties shall meet and confer as to the exact date and time of the deposition and inform the court in writing either of the date and time upon which they agreed, or that they haven't agreed and on what date and time each of them wants the deposition to proceed. If the parties do not agree upon a date and time and so inform the court by January 4, 2013, then the court will select a date and time. Sanctions are denied. The court believes that a better meet and confer effort by both sides would likely have avoided this motion over scheduling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt
